exampleSentences: Anh thích cà phê hơn trà.
notes:
hơn means “more” or “than” when comparing two things. It shows that one thing has a higher degree than another.

It comes after an adjective or verb in comparisons.  

With adjectives, it means “more + adjective” (đắt hơn = more expensive). With verbs, it shows preference or greater action (thích hơn = prefer more).

Opposite idea: kém hơn (less than). For equality, use bằng (as…as).
sentenceBreakdowns: Anh (he) + thích (like) + cà phê (coffee) + hơn (than) + trà (tea)
notes_md:
**hơn** means **“more” or “than”** when comparing two things. It shows that one thing has a higher degree than another.

It comes **after an adjective or verb** in comparisons.  

With adjectives, it means “more + adjective” (đắt hơn = more expensive). With verbs, it shows preference or greater action (thích hơn = prefer more).

Opposite idea: **kém hơn** (less than). For equality, use **bằng** (as…as).

example.vi: Tôi học tiếng Anh hơn năm nay.
example.en: I have been studying English for more than a year.
example.es: He estado estudiando inglés durante más de un año.

definitions.en: Used to indicate a greater degree or amount.
definitions.es: Se utiliza para indicar un mayor grado o cantidad.
definitions.vi: Được sử dụng để chỉ mức độ hoặc số lượng lớn hơn.

exampleSentencesTranslated: He likes coffee more than tea.
She eats rice more than noodles.
I like cats more than dogs.
